# Kanban skill

> A Claude Code skill that runs a kanban board from plain Markdown files in your
> repo — versioned in git, no database, no MCP. Claude proposes the next work,
> writes the cards, and archives what's done; the board lives as plain Markdown
> in `docs/kanban/`, diffable and readable by you and the agent.

Each page below has a plain-Markdown mirror (the `.md` links) with the full page
content — no navigation, styling, or scripts.

## Pages

- [Home — your task board in Markdown, right next to your code](https://kanbanskill.cc/index.md): What the skill is, how to install it in one prompt, driving the board in plain language, the local board UI, the indie-hacker preset, and Markdown project management (recurring tasks, group tasks, project memory, metrics).
- [Kanban skill vs. GitHub Issues](https://kanbanskill.cc/vs-github-issues.md): A head-to-head across storage, token cost, agent ergonomics, teams, and transparency — a local Markdown scratch-board vs. a shared, public system of record, and when to use each.
- [Kanban skill vs. Hermes Agent Kanban](https://kanbanskill.cc/vs-hermes-kanban.md): Two overlapping agent kanban boards — plain diffable files that run on any agent (even Hermes) vs. a durable, shared SQLite queue many named agents claim tasks from. Covers harness support, memory vs. audit, autonomy level, dashboards, and trade-offs.

## Recipes

- [Daily kanban maintenance](https://kanbanskill.cc/recipes/daily-kanban-maintenance.md): A recurring upkeep task — each pass proposes new work, prunes stale cards, and keeps priorities short, so the board stays sharp on its own.
- [Competitor analysis loop](https://kanbanskill.cc/recipes/competitor-analysis-loop.md): A recurring competitor-intelligence task powered by dist0 — pull the competitors people name in your Reddit signal, study each real one deeply, and file build/grow/offer ideas as cards.

## More

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/dist0com/kanban-skill): Source, install prompt, and the skill files.
